-- 1 of 5 --

The Quick Launch Part 2
!
Let’s walk through the quick launch.
!
I have three examples I’m going to walk you through. They’re very different examples,
but you’ll see how the quick launch can work in a lot of different ways.
The first one is this classic tax sale. I did this years and years ago, but it keeps on
working. I just heard from two Product Launch Formula owners that have done almost
this exact same thing in the last couple of months.
!
I did it as a tax sale, you can go ahead and use this example to do it as a tax sale
yourself. But you could do it around any event in your life. One Product Launch Formula
owner did it as, “My pipes burst.” He went away on vacation, when he came back the
pipes in his house had burst and he needed to make a lot of money to pay for the
plumber to come in and fix it. You can use it in a lot of different ways, the tax sale is
just one example.
!
I’m going to walk you through this and you’re going to see the timing was very quick
and it required very little effort. It was basically four emails and a landing page. I came
up with the idea, I implemented it the very next day, and it was ultra quick.
!
The offer in this case was that I gave a discount off my normal price. The triggers I hit
were the reason why, a story, scarcity, and I had an advanced on that I haven’t
mentioned so far in the course yet that we call future pacing. That’s not really a trigger,
it’s more like a copy technique. That’s sort of the background on the tax sale. Let’s get
right to exactly how I did it.
!
The fun thing about this is I actually captured some of my thinking in this internal
memo. This was not a marketing piece, this was basically an email I sent to my partner
in this business explaining this idea that I had. I actually sent this on April 12 th and
you’ll see we started the sale on April 14 th
, so it took all of two days. I was telling my
partner that I got this nasty surprise on my taxes and I need to come up with $25,000.
Not that I needed to come up with it, but that I would like to come up with it. I said,
“Let’s use it as a reason why.” You can basically see my thinking here and this was
literally on the afternoon of April 12 th and you’ll see it didn’t take too long to put
together the tax sale.
!
This was the first email I sent out on the morning of April 14 th
, so it was about 36 to 48
hours after I came up with the idea. These are the triggers I hit here in this email;
story, reason why, and scarcity.
!
Subject line: Jeff’s Huge Tax Bill
!
Copyright © All Rights Reserved Internet Alchemy, Inc. 2014 -2015 	Page ! 2
PLF_Module1v1.0

-- 2 of 5 --

It was just very conversational. You’ll notice my copy is always very conversation. I tell
them it’s a little embarrassing, I got a nasty surprise and I’m going to have write a big
check. This is all just story based stuff.
!
More story based stuff here.
!
Here, bottom line, I’m putting this on sale, it’s an IRS sale, we don’t normally discount
trading subscriptions but now we’re going to offer a discount.
!
Here I’m already started to build scarcity. It’s April 14 th and I’m sending this to them
and they only have until April 16 th
.
!
Basically here I explain the offer and then I send them right to the order page. In this
case, there wasn’t any prelaunch at all, we took them straight to an order page right off
a reason why email.
!
Here this is sort of talking about the current market. This is for the stock market
traders, but up here it was completely 100% all about this story. Remember those
triggers I’m hitting; the story, the reason why, and the scarcity. Here we tie it a little bit
back to the topic for the market that we’re selling into, but you can see the pitch is
mostly about the story.
Here telling them this is the best price ever. That’s sort of setting the hook on the
scarcity.
!
Here’s the second email that we sent out. One of the things that we used to do is each
day we would send this update out. This update would go out the day after we
published it to our real time traders. That was the way we marketed.
!
Every day we would publish this update to our paid subscribers and then the next day
we would send this out to our prospect list. It was the exact same email, but I would
have a built-in marketing message and I would put it up at the top here and I would
put in the middle. This is how we marketed every single day, so every day they got an
email that we had sent out the night before to our paid subscribers.
!
One thing I want to mention is this red in here is just for your benefit so you could see
this, so that it stands out a little bit. When we actually published this is was not in red,
it was just pure email.
!
At the top of the message I just said, “sale, sale, sale,” and we’re already hitting them
with scarcity, “There’s just one day left.”
!
You can see this is a long time ago, but this technique still works. Like I said, in the last
couple of months I’ve had two Product Launch Formula owners that basically used this
exact same campaign and had it work for them. This technique is going to work 30
years from now.
!

-- 3 of 5 --

We came down here, we recapped how we did in this update, and then basically it’s
scarcity, “Just 24 hours left,” referring to the IRS sale, bringing that story back in.
!
This was sent April 14 th
...This is sent April 15 th
...Now this goes out April 16 th
, very
similar to what we did the day before. In fact, it’s almost identical. The only thing I did
was I really changed this top part. Instead of “sale, sale, sale,” it said, “IRS sale.”
!
Came down here and basically did the same thing. This was, “Boy, you should have
jumped on board, because you would have made some money already.”
Then we came down to big time scarcity, “We’re down to eight hours left.” We’re
sending them directly to the landing page.
!
Then on the same day, April 16 th
, we came back later on and this was a dedicated
email. These previous two emails went out as part of our normal marketing message,
this was another dedicated email. This was only the second dedicated email. We sent
one out on the 14 th
. This is the fourth email in the series, but it’s only the second
dedicated email. This one came out just a few hours after the third one.
!
Here’s the fourth email. We haven’t talked about this in the course yet, but we will. This
is a bit of the reluctant hero. I’m getting angry email from someone who missed the
deadline for our IRS sale. This is a little of that future pacing, this is where we try to get
people to walk forward in time and imagine where they would be if they didn’t purchase
from us and the negatives to that.
!
This is basically, “If we don’t give you that final warning, someone is going to miss out
on the savings and they’re going to get really torque off, and then I’m going to get a
nasty email. This way I send a final warning and I’m covered.” That’s a bit of future
pacing in there. That can be a really powerful technique. It’s sort of an advanced copy
technique where you’re just walking someone forward and showing them the
consequences if they don’t take any action.
!
Scarcity, scarcity, scarcity. “You just have eight hours. You’ve already heard my IRS
saga.” Even though I’m telling them I’m not going to repeat it, by referring back to it I
am tapping into the story and the reason why again.
!
Then we go down and I talk about the guarantee. That’s email number four.
Here I want to show you the landing page that we would drop people on.
!
It’s very simple, it’s just IRS Sale and playing into the story, “Jeff screwed up his tax
planning and he needs to raise cash fast, so he’s putting this real time updates on sale
for ridiculous prices.” I just repeat the story here, repeat the offer, repeat the scarcity.
We come down and we give them a bunch of testimonials. That’s about it.
!
This is incredibly simple promotion. We just had the original reason why, a couple of
reminders, then a final scarcity message, and we dropped them on this super simple
landing page. This landing page was just I took my normal order page and I put up
Copyright © All Rights Reserved Internet Alchemy, Inc. 2014 -2015 	Page ! 4

-- 4 of 5 --

some additional text. Total time invested in this is probably two or three hours and we
made a boatload of money, it was very effective.
!
Like I mentioned, it has been used both in this form as a tax sale and it’s been used
recently with great success by my Product Launch Formula owners. Also, you can use it
as a “my pipes burst” sale, or any type of thing that went wrong, some unexpected
thing that went wrong.
!
You want to avoid looking like it’s a begathon. You never want to be begging, that’s not
an attractive thing to do. If you can use a little bit of humor, like I did here, I wasn’t
begging at all. I threw in, “I screwed up,” and a little bit of humor. This type of sale can
be very effective and you can do it without hurting the perceived value of your offer.
!
!
